title: ID Evidence API overview
text: View and verify the evidence of your recipient's identity. The ID Evidence API enables Docusign customers using [ID Verification (IDV)](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/how-to/id-verification/) for a recipient to retrieve the data, including picture ID, used to identify the recipient during signing.

title: Go-Live
text:
When you are ready to launch your ID Evidence API app in production, you will need to promote your application’s integration key from your developer account to a production Docusign account by passing a Go-Live review, similar to the [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/go-live/) process for the eSignature API.

Before you can begin the Go-Live process, you must have a paid production Docusign account with a plan that includes ID Evidence.

When you are ready to start the Go-Live review for your application, follow the steps described on the [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/go-live/) overview page for Docusign eSignature.

**Note:** If your application fails Go-Live review, you may be required to bring it into compliance with the [API rules and resource limits](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/rules-and-limits/) before you can Go-Live.

After the form is processed (which takes up to three business days), your integration key will be copied into production, enabling your app to call the production ID Evidence API endpoints.

title: Go-Live
text:
The developer environment and production endpoints for most Docusign APIs use slightly different paths. For the ID Evidence API:

| Environment | ID Evidence API base URL |
| --- | --- |
| Developer | https://proof-d.docusign.net/api/ |
| Production | https://proof.docusign.net/api/ |

**Note:** {server} is the data center location of your production account (for example, **CA**, **NA2**, or **EU**). You can obtain your production data center location from:

- The `base_uri` in the response of a call to [/oauth/userinfo](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/user-info/). If you obtain the `base_uri` in this way, you should do it once when the user authenticates for the first time and cache the response.
- The **Account Base URI** section of the [Apps and Keys](https://admindemo.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=appsAndKeys) page

For a full list of sites, see [Site certificates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=khf1655309726546&topicId=kkh1657307586557.html&_LANG=enus). To access production API endpoints, you'll need to enable your integration key in the production environment. See [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/go-live/) for more information.

title: How-to guides overview
text:
The how-to guides in this section demonstrate how common [ID Evidence](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/idevidence101/) scenarios work and how you can implement them into your own apps.

All API how-to guides assume you are developing with a Docusign developer account. A developer account is not specific to the [eSignature REST API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/). If you have a developer account that you are using with our eSignature REST API, it will also work with other [Docusign APIs](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/). If you need to create a free developer account, select **Developer Account** from the menu at the top-right of the page, then **Create Account** or use the button below.

[Create account](https://www.docusign.com/developers/sandbox)

Each ID Evidence API how-to guide is listed below:

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence events
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence events
text: This topic demonstrates how to retrieve ID Evidence event data in JSON format.

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence events
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence events
text:
- If you do not see a sidebar link named **Identity Verification** under eSignature Settings in your Docusign account, you do not have ID Verification (IDV) enabled. See [ID verification](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/recipients/auth/#idv) for information on enabling IDV in your account.
- You must [enable ID Evidence storage](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/idevidence101/enabling-idevidence-storage/).
- In your Docusign account, you must have a completed IDV envelope: you must have [sent an IDV envelope](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/how-to/id-verification/), the recipient must have [completed the IDV process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=iww1578456547699.html), and they must have signed the envelope.
- In your locally downloaded Quickstart or launcher `/``config` folder, you must save your IDV envelope ID GUID in a file named `IDV_ENVELOPE_ID`.

Run this code example from our [Quickstart](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/quickstart/) or launcher projects: [Bash](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-bash), [PowerShell](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-powershell).

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence events
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence events
text:
Running the code in this how-to requires this data:

| Data element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| {API\_ACCOUNT\_ID} | A [GUID](http://guid.one/) value that identifies your account. This value is automatically generated by Docusign for any account you create. Copy the value from the **API Account ID** field on the [Apps and Keys](https://admindemo.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=appsAndKeys) page. |
| {BASE\_PATH} | A string value that forms the root of the URL required to make API calls. The base path value in the example code is set to target the developer environment at  https://<domain>/restapi  Where <domain> is demo.docusign.net for the developer environment and is the result of the GetUserInfo call in production. |
| {IDV\_ENVELOPE\_ID} | A [GUID](http://guid.one/) value that identifies the [Docusign envelope completed using ID Verification](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/how-to/id-verification/). |
| {PROOF\_BASE\_PATH} | A string value that forms the root of the URL required to make API calls. The base path value in the example code is set to target the developer environment at  https://<domain>/api  Where <domain> is proof-d.docusign.net for the developer environment and {server}.proof.docusign.net for production. |
| {RECIPIENT\_ID\_GUID} | A [GUID](http://guid.one/) value that Docusign assigns to identify each recipient in an envelope. This value is globally unique for all recipients, not just those in your account. |

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
text: This topic demonstrates how to retrieve the image of an ID stored as part of the ID Verification (IDV) process. To retrieve such an image, you need its URL, which is part of the JSON response when you [retrieve ID Evidence events](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/retrieve-idevidence-events/#expected-response).

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
text:
- If you do not see a sidebar link named **Identity Verification** under eSignature Settings in your Docusign account, you do not have ID Verification (IDV) enabled. See [ID verification](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/recipients/auth/#idv) for information on enabling IDV in your account.
- You must [enable ID Evidence storage](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/idevidence101/enabling-idevidence-storage/).
- In your Docusign account, you must have a completed IDV envelope: you must have [sent an IDV envelope](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/how-to/id-verification/), the recipient must have [completed the IDV process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=iww1578456547699.html), and they must have signed the envelope.
- In your locally downloaded Quickstart or launcher `/``config` folder, you must save your IDV envelope ID GUID in a file named `IDV_ENVELOPE_ID`.

Run this code example from our [Quickstart](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/quickstart/) or launcher projects: [Bash](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-bash), [PowerShell](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-powershell).

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
text:
Running the code in this how-to requires this data:

| Data element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| {API\_ACCOUNT\_ID} | A [GUID](http://guid.one/) value that identifies your account. This value is automatically generated by Docusign for any account you create. Copy the value from the **API Account ID** field on the [Apps and Keys](https://admindemo.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=appsAndKeys) page. |
| {BASE\_PATH} | A string value that forms the root of the URL required to make API calls. The base path value in the example code is set to target the developer environment at  https://<domain>/restapi  Where <domain> is demo.docusign.net for the developer environment and is the result of the GetUserInfo call in production. |
| {COPY\_OF\_ID\_FRONT\_URL} | A URL associated with the front of a photo ID media image. The media URL will be of the following format:  {PROOF\_BASE\_PATH}/api/v1/events/person/{RECIPIENT\_ID\_GUID}/{EVENT\_ID}/media/{MEDIA\_ID}  For information on retrieving an ID Evidence event list containing a media image URL, see [How to retrieve ID Evidence events](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/retrieve-idevidence-events/). |
| {IDV\_ENVELOPE\_ID} | A [GUID](http://guid.one/) value that identifies the [Docusign envelope completed using ID Verification](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/how-to/id-verification/). |
| {PROOF\_BASE\_PATH} | A string value that forms the root of the URL required to make API calls. The base path value in the example code is set to target the developer environment at  https://<domain>/api  Where <domain> is proof-d.docusign.net for the developer environment and {server}.proof.docusign.net for production. |
| {RECIPIENT\_ID\_GUID} | A [GUID](http://guid.one/) value that Docusign assigns to identify each recipient in an envelope. This value is globally unique for all recipients, not just those in your account. |

title: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
heading_path: How to retrieve ID Evidence media
text:
If you run this code example from our Quickstart or launcher projects, you should see a text file saved to your local directory that contains the image of the front of your photo ID encoded in Base64 format.You will need to use a Base64 decoder to convert the string into a binary image that can be displayed to the user
 **Note**: The image of the front of your photo ID will have a honeycomb pattern superimposed on it and cannot be used to make additional IDV requests.

title: ID Evidence API 101
text:
The ID Evidence API enables Docusign customers using [ID Verification (IDV)](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/how-to/id-verification/) to retrieve the data that can be stored while verifying a signer’s ID. By default, no personal data is stored with IDV. The only data that is stored is the status of the ID verification, transaction number, and the name of the third-party ID verifier. A recipient (signer) is considered to be authenticated if the government-issued ID is validated, but you must configure which data fields Docusign is authorized to store as ID evidence, if you want to retrieve personal data afterwards (see [Enabling ID Evidence storage](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/idevidence101/enabling-idevidence-storage/)).

Two categories of API calls can be made with the ID Evidence API:

- **Events:** Log of all state changes to an envelope’s recipient during the ID verification process; stores the selected text-based data, such as name, date of birth, and more.
- **Media:** Retrieval of configured ID verification image data that was captured for a corresponding event. For example, media is stored for an event that is generated as a result of a recipient’s passport verification.

**Note:** When working with the ID Evidence API, you’ll notice the name of some endpoints referring to proof. This is because data stored as part of the IDV process is proof that the ID was verified.

Every time a sender posts an envelope that requires ID Verification, Docusign calls the ID Evidence API to initiate a new proof file for each recipient, which is used to store data. Once the transaction is initiated, an Envelope Sent event is triggered and pushed to the ID Evidence API, along with its media (data). When an envelope recipient uploads their ID, ID Verification pushes a new Upload Image event to the envelope's proof file. ID Verification continues to log events with every transactional state change.

For more information about ID Evidence, see the [ID Evidence Q&A](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=mrc1578885288021.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Enable ID Evidence storage
text: To store images of the government-issued IDs captured by the ID verification (IDV) process for each signer, you must have the **Front side of the ID** and **Back side of the ID** options selected.

title: Enable ID Evidence storage
heading_path: Verify and edit your IDV configuration
text:
1. In Docusign eSignature Settings, select **Identity Verification**.
   1. If you do not see a sidebar link named **Identity Verification** under eSignature Settings in your Docusign account, you do not have ID Verification (IDV) enabled. See [ID verification](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/recipients/auth/#idv) for information on enabling IDV in your account.
2. Locate your **Docusign ID Verification** configuration from the list.
   1. If your **Docusign ID Verification** configuration has **Read-only** permission, [create a new IDV configuration](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/idevidence101/enabling-idevidence-storage/#create-a-new-idv-configuration).
3. Select **ACTIONS** for the IDV configuration you want to edit and select **Edit**.
4. Under the **Data to save from each Photo ID or Passport verification** section, make sure  that **Front side of the ID** and **Back side of the ID** are selected as shown in the image.
   1. If they are not selected, select the two boxes as per the image.
5. Select **SAVE**.

title: Enable ID Evidence storage
text:
1. In Docusign eSignature Settings, select **Identity Verification**.
2. Select **ADD CONFIGURATION**.
3. Enter a name (and optional description) for the configuration.
4. For the verification method, select **Docusign ID Verification** from the dropdown menu.
5. Under the **Data to save from each Photo ID or Passport verification** section, select **Front side of the ID** and **Back side of the ID** as shown in the image.
6. Select **SAVE**.
7. [Add your new IDV configuration to a recipient](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/idevidence101/enabling-idevidence-storage/#add-your-new-idv-configuration-to-a-recipient).

title: Enable ID Evidence storage
heading_path: Add your new IDV configuration to a recipient
text:
1. Start a new envelope and add documents and recipients as usual.
2. For the recipient for whom you want to request identity verification, select **CUSTOMIZE** then select **Add identity verification**.
3. Select your new IDV configuration from the list of **Identity Verification** methods.
4. Finish preparing your envelope as usual and send it.

title: Resource tokens
text:
The Docusign ID Evidence API uses [OAUTH Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/) to make secure API calls just as all Docusign APIs do. However, the ID Evidence API takes this one step further to secure access to the specific resources within an account with a resource token.

An OAuth token is used to request a resource token, which you then supply as a bearer token in the next request to ID Evidence to retrieve events or media. A resource token is valid for 2 minutes. For more information about how to use resource tokens and the ID Evidence API to retrieve events and media, see the [ID Evidence How-tos](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/).

text:
If you're using ID Verification to verify the identity of your recipients, you can use the ID Evidence API to store the identification data you want. You can also export stored data, such as first name, last name, date of birth, expiration date, or copies of scanned IDs to various formats.

[Events](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/reference/idevidence/events/)

Description

Events pushed to ID Evidence.

title: : getMedia
text: Gets the media item related to a specific event. This call is built using the response to the `list Events` request (see the `include_media` parameter).

title: : getMedia
text:
- [How to retrieve ID Evidence events](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/retrieve-idevidence-events/)
- [How to retrieve ID Evidence media](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/retrieve-idevidence-media/)

title: : getMedia
text:
GET

```
/restapi/api/v1.0/events/{entityType}/{entityId}/{eventId}/media/{mediaId}
```

title: : getMedia
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| eventId \* | string | The id of the event as recorded in the database. Optional. |
| mediaId \* | string | The id of the media associated with the event. Optional. |
| entityType \* | string | The type of the entity (for example room, user or envelope). ID Evidence only supports the `person` (recipient) entity type. Optional. |
| entityId \* | string | The id of the Docusign entity. Optional. |
| version \* | string | The requested API version |

\* Required

title: : list
text:
Returns a list of events for a specific recipient. You can filter the results by specifying additional criteria.
**Note**: This method supersedes the older `GET /api/v1.0/events/{entityType}/{entityId}` request. If you're still using the old `GET` request, we highly recommend you switch to this method.

title: : list
text:
- [How to retrieve ID Evidence events](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/retrieve-idevidence-events/)
- [How to retrieve ID Evidence media](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/idevidence-api/how-to/retrieve-idevidence-media/)

title: : list
text:
GET

```
/restapi/api/v1.0/events/{entityType}/{entityId}.{format}
```

title: : list
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| entityType \* | string | The type of the entity (for example room, user or envelope). ID Evidence only supports the `person` (recipient) entity type. Optional. |
| entityId \* | string | The id of the Docusign entity. Optional. |
| format \* | string | The format of the content in the response. ID Evidence supports the following format and extensions:   - JSON (json) - PDF (pdf)   Note: if you choose the PDF format, ID Evidence applies a digital signature to the file to guarantee the origin and integrity of data. |
| version \* | string | The requested API version |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| actor\_id | string | *This parameter is not used with ID Evidence.* |
| from\_date | string | Retrieves all the events created from the specified date. This date is inclusive. If empty, then all the events created up until the *to\_date* value are returned. |
| to\_date | string | Retrieves all the events created until the specified date. This date is inclusive. If empty, then all the events created from the from\_date are returned. |
| include\_media | boolean | Indicates whether you want to include the media in the response. The media depends on the event returned by the ID Evidence API. For example, ID Evidence can return the capture of the ID uploaded for verification. Note that including the media in the response may carry a large amount of data. If set to *false*, then the request returns a link that you can use later with the Get Media request to retrieve the media file. |
| page\_index | integer | The index position of the currently displayed page. |
| page\_size | integer | The number of records to display on a page. |

\* Required
